forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,55 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a180
+project: diagnostics
+title: 'code-context: indexing leadership must belong to a live/current process (stale leader wedges the index forever; periodic poll is dead code behind it)'
+---
+# code-context: leadership is immortal — a stale leader wedges indexing forever
+
+## The real bug (verified live in calcutron, Jun 20 2026)
+
+The index fixes already shipped — ^hdcwqk6 (watcher Created/Modified UPSERT so new files enter the dirty set) and ^4ps51p9 (leader runs `startup_cleanup` glob re-walk every 5 min, then drains `WHERE ts_indexed = 0`) — are **correct and wired** (verified end-to-end: server.rs:676 `run_periodic_reconcile` is leader-only; the drain `index_discovered_files_with_embedder` writes `ts_chunks` and flips `ts_indexed=1`). They are exactly the "file notifications + periodic glob double-check like at index start" design.
+
+But they are **dead code in practice**, because of a deeper bug they sit behind:
+
+**Leadership is acquired by the first process to grab an `flock` and is held until that process dies. There is no lease, heartbeat, generation, or takeover. A leader is decided ONCE at init and never re-evaluated. So a `sah serve` from a finished/abandoned session holds workspace indexing leadership forever, and the live/current session can never take over.**
+
+### Live evidence
+- Lock `…/T/code-context-ts-bc3927cb….lock` holds **PID 9852** — a `sah serve` started **Jun 19 14:18**, still alive 25h+ later. Its parent `claude` (PID 8273, a Jun-19 root session) never exited, so 9852's stdin never hits EOF, so 9852 never exits, so the flock never frees.
+- `grep -c reconcile mcp.9852.log` = **0** → 9852 runs pre-fix code; it logs the old failure live: `N file change(s) detected, marking dirty` → `0 dirty, 0 deleted` → `no dirty files to index`.
+- Every current-session process (root + subagents) logs `not the leader for this workspace (leader is PID 9852)` and joins as a follower. The follower re-election loop (`spawn_reelection_loop`, server.rs ~556) calls `try_promote()` every 5s, but `try_promote` only succeeds when the flock is **free** (election.rs ~500-543, `try_lock_exclusive` → `EWOULDBLOCK` while held). A live-but-stale leader is never displaceable.
+- `index.db` frozen at the Jun-19 snapshot: `indexed_files=1` (only `demo.sh`), while `src/`+`tests/` hold 7 real `.rs` files. Rebuilding the binary changes nothing because a rebuild cannot replace a running process, and new code cannot break 9852's flock.
+
+## Architecture map (from reading the code)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs`: pure `flock` via `fs2::try_lock_exclusive`; writes leader PID to the lock file as a **diagnostic only** (the flock is the source of truth). No TTL/heartbeat/generation. `try_promote()` only checks if the lock is free; no staleness detection, no preemption.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`: `do_initialize_code_context` snapshots `is_leader` ONCE (~line 376) and gates LSP-supervisor + worker spawn on it. Leaders run NO re-election loop; followers poll every 5s and the loop EXITS permanently after a successful promote.
+- Process lifetime: `sah serve` blocks on `running_service.waiting()` (stdin EOF) — `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs` ~936-971. It only exits when its MCP client disconnects (stdin EOF) or on signal. A long-lived parent `claude` keeps it alive indefinitely.
+- No notion of "root" vs "subagent" session anywhere in election.
+
+## Desired behavior (per the user)
+- The **live/current root session's** sah is the indexing leader and runs the periodic poll.
+- **Subagent sah servers never lead** — they always join as followers and ask the leader. (User explicitly confirmed: "subagents never lead.")
+- A leader from a finished/abandoned session must NOT be able to wedge the workspace; a live session must be able to reclaim leadership.
+
+## Fix direction (decide details during implementation; keep it as simple as correctness allows)
+Make leadership reclaimable by a live process instead of an immortal flock grant. Candidate mechanism:
+- **Lease + heartbeat:** the leader renews a timestamp (in the lock/leader file) on an interval; the lease — not the raw flock — becomes the takeover authority. A candidate that sees a stale lease (no/old heartbeat, e.g. a leader that died, hung, or runs code that never heartbeats) atomically claims leadership (O_EXCL/rename CAS), and the displaced leader steps down (stops indexing, becomes follower) to preserve single-writer. A healthy leader keeps leading and serving followers — which is fine and matches the model.
+- **Subagents never lead:** gate election entry so only a root/top-level `sah serve` contends; subagent-spawned servers always follow. Requires a reliable root-vs-subagent signal — verify one exists (harness env var, or process ancestry) before relying on it.
+- Optionally: `sah serve` should exit promptly when its session ends (stdin EOF / parent death) so a finished leader frees the flock on its own.
+
+## Hard constraint to call out
+This change cannot retroactively fix the **currently** wedged leader (PID 9852): old code will not cooperate with any new lease/heartbeat protocol, so that one process must be cycled once. After that, with new code everywhere, the wedge never recurs. Document this in the PR.
+
+## Tests
+- Unit (deterministic, no model): simulate two "sessions" against one workspace lock dir — leader A acquires + heartbeats; candidate B cannot take over while A's lease is fresh; when A's heartbeat goes stale (simulate by not renewing / advancing a injected clock), B takes over and A, on its next tick, observes loss and steps down. Assert exactly one leader throughout.
+- Unit: a subagent-flagged server never acquires leadership even when the lock is free (always follows).
+- Keep unit tests <10s; any real-process/real-flock fidelity test goes in `tests/`.
+
+## Anchors
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs` (`elect`, `try_promote`, lock-file write ~164-185, path derivation ~231-245)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` (`do_initialize_code_context` is_leader snapshot ~376, `spawn_reelection_loop` ~556, `spawn_ts_and_watcher_workers` ~628, `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` ~700)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/unified_server.rs` (~936-971 stdin-EOF lifetime)
+- Live evidence: `calcutron/.sah/mcp.9852.log`; lock `…/T/code-context-ts-bc3927cb1cd02e5607b40c650bbd4c3b.lock`; index `calcutron/.code-context/index.db`
+- Related (DONE, correct, sit behind this): ^hdcwqk6 (watcher UPSERT), ^4ps51p9 (periodic glob reconcile). Leader epic: 01KV86XGD5ZFS1F6VQ47A5H2BJ (leader-only LSP spawn), 01KVDEGQ75R48YNFE76X6M3JPZ (no-orphan kill-on-exit).
\ No newline at end of file

Leader renews on heartbeat; a leader that finds the lease stolen (nonce changed) steps down. Inject a clock (TimeSource) for deterministic <10s unit tests. Two concurrent followers over a stale lease would BOTH pass the staleness check and BOTH write -> BOTH return Won (different nonces) -> BOTH bring up proxy/indexing = a two-writer window until the loser''s next heartbeat (~5s). That violates the task''s hard invariant "at most one leader, no two-writer window during takeover." The agent''s other deviation (in-place truncate+write instead of temp+rename, to preserve the flock''d inode) is a CORRECT insight and I''m keeping it. Dispatched a surgical fix: add CLAIM_TTL + an O_EXCL claim-marker gate (a SEPARATE <lock>.claim file, never the flock''d inode) inside try_claim_lease so exactly one concurrent candidate proceeds to write the lease, plus the missing test_concurrent_claims_yield_exactly_one_winner and test_stale_claim_marker_is_reclaimed. Verifying.'

A just-created marker always has a recent mtime so concurrent candidates always see it FRESH and back off; only a marker with mtime older than CLAIM_TTL (a crashed claimer) is reclaimed. Reclaim test updated to age the marker mtime via filetime (already a workspace dep). Requiring a 30x robustness run to prove the gate is genuinely race-free, not just usually-green.'

Final mechanism summary:
- LEASE = JSON {pid, nonce, heartbeat_ms} written in-place into the flock''d lock file (in-place truncate-after-write preserves the flock inode; rename would orphan the per-inode flock). HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL=5s, LEASE_TTL=15s, CLAIM_TTL=2s.
- TAKEOVER: candidate reads lease; FRESH -> follower (never preempt); STALE -> claim. Single-winner enforced by an O_EXCL <lock>.claim marker (separate file, never the flock''d inode), staleness keyed on the marker''s OS-stamped MTIME (not content, not injected clock) to avoid the torn-read race I caught; under-gate re-check before writing. - FLOCK FAST PATH now defers to a FRESH lease (Finding 2 fix): a free flock does not entitle takeover if a live lease-leader exists.
- STEP-DOWN (single-writer enforcement, Finding 1 fix): WorkspaceMode::Leader owns a shared ShutdownFlag (reusing the existing crate ShutdownFlag/lsp_worker mechanism). step_down() and Drop set it BEFORE dropping the leader guard; the SAME flag is threaded into ALL leader writers — TS indexer (one-shot guard), file watcher (select! 500ms tick, checks at loop-top + before write), periodic reconcile (checks each tick), and LSP indexing workers (replacing the throwaway flag) — at all three entry points (initial-leader TS+LSP, post-promotion TS+LSP). A preempted leader stops writing before the new leader is sole writer; no two-writer window.

+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-20 19:10)
+
+Scope: 6 files of the leadership-reclaim change (lease.rs, policy.rs, election.rs, workspace.rs, server.rs, watcher.rs). Concurrency invariants verified directly against the code, not taken from comments.
+
+### Blockers
+- [ ] **Two-writer window on takeover — the TS indexer ignores the step-down flag (Invariant 3 VIOLATED).** The tree-sitter indexer is the leader's primary DB write path, but `index_discovered_files_with_embedder` / `index_discovered_files_async` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs` take only `(workspace_root, db, [embedder,] reporter)` — they do **not** receive the `ShutdownFlag`, so the flag cannot be (and is not) checked inside the `for relative_path in &dirty_files` per-file write loop. The flag is checked only ONCE *before* the call: `spawn_ts_and_watcher_workers` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` checks `ts_shutdown.load(..)` before invoking the indexer, and `watcher.rs` checks before invoking. A full pass is "minutes-to-tens-of-minutes" per the function's own docstring (mod.rs ~1840). Consequence: when a live session takes over a stale lease via `try_promote_via_lease`, the new (different) process starts its own writers immediately, while the preempted old leader only learns it lost on its next heartbeat — and any in-flight TS pass on the old leader keeps writing the same on-disk SQLite DB through its entire remaining dirty set, ignoring `step_down()`'s flag. The cross-process `SharedDb` mutex does NOT serialize these (it is per-process). This is the exact single-writer guarantee the whole change exists to preserve. **Fix:** thread the `ShutdownFlag` into `index_discovered_files_async` / `index_discovered_files_with_embedder` and check it at the top of the `for relative_path in &dirty_files` loop (before the per-file write block), returning early when set — mirroring `run_lsp_indexing_loop`'s per-iteration check in `lsp_worker.rs`. Update both call sites (server.rs and watcher.rs) to pass the flag the workers already hold.
+- [ ] **No `PRAGMA busy_timeout` under WAL amplifies the overlap into silent corruption (secondary, same root cause).** `configure_connection` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/db.rs` ~110-117) enables WAL but sets no `busy_timeout`. During any residual cross-process write overlap, the second writer gets immediate `SQLITE_BUSY`, and worker writes are `let _ = conn.execute(...)` with the error swallowed — so a losing writer's chunk writes can be dropped while the row is still flipped `ts_indexed = 1`, i.e. silent index corruption. Set a `PRAGMA busy_timeout` so an unavoidable residual overlap retries rather than silently dropping writes. (Primary fix is the flag check above; this is defense-in-depth.)
+
+### Invariants verified to HOLD (no action needed — recorded for the implementer)
+- [x] **Invariant 1 — at most one leader / takeover admits exactly one winner.** `try_claim_lease` (lease.rs) gates the rewrite on `acquire_claim_marker` using `OpenOptions::create_new(true)` (O_EXCL) as the atomic single-winner gate, then re-reads the lease under the gate and bails if a heartbeat made it fresh (closes the read→claim TOCTOU). Staleness is keyed on OS-set mtime, not torn file contents (the comment correctly explains a content check would admit two winners). `test_concurrent_claims_yield_exactly_one_winner` (8 threads) asserts exactly one win.
+- [x] **Invariant 2 — fresh-lease leader never preempted (incl. flock fast-path).** `try_claim_lease` returns `Lost` on a non-stale lease before contending. `FollowerGuard::try_promote` does not treat a free flock as entitlement: after winning the flock it reads the lease and, if fresh, drops the flock and returns `Ok(None)` without deleting the file. `test_try_promote_flock_free_but_fresh_lease_does_not_preempt` covers it. Staleness uses `saturating_sub` (backward clock = treated fresh) and a strict `> TTL` boundary, tested at exactly-TTL.
+- [x] **Invariant 4 — preempted old leader cannot delete/overwrite the new leader's lease.** `LeaderGuard::Drop` deletes the lock file only when `self.holds_flock && lease::lease_held_by(&self.lock_path, self.nonce)` — a nonce ownership re-check, so a stepped-down old leader leaves the winner's lease intact. `test_preempted_leader_drop_preserves_new_leader_lease` confirms.
+- [x] **Invariant 5 — lease write is in-place (no rename).** `write_lease_atomic` opens the existing path (`create(true).truncate(false)`), `seek(0)`, `write_all`, `set_len` — same inode, no temp-then-rename. Docstring documents why rename would orphan the per-inode flock. Heartbeat and initial-lease writes both flow through this one function.
+- [x] **Invariant 6 — heartbeat-loss (nonce mismatch) triggers step-down + re-election.** `lease::heartbeat` returns `false` on nonce mismatch / missing file / write failure; `LeaderGuard::heartbeat` → `heartbeat_lease` propagates it; `spawn_leader_heartbeat_loop` (server.rs) acts on `false` — logs, calls `ws.step_down()`, spawns the re-election poll, and breaks. Not ignored.
+- [x] **git_sha INFO log intact.** `\"code-context: elected leader\"` is present in server.rs, emitted with structured fields in the leader arm (from commit 99a11eabb).
+- [x] **Known partial NOT flagged.** The \"subagents never lead\" policy gate (`SAH_SUBAGENT=1` + TODO in policy.rs) is the documented, accepted inert follow-up; the lease mechanism makes it an optimization, not a correctness requirement.
+
+### Warnings (engine fleet — non-blocking)
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs` — `open_as_leader` and `become_leader_with_guard` near-verbatim duplicate the open→configure→schema→startup-cleanup→`Arc<Mutex>` sequence. Extract a shared `init_leader_db(db_path, workspace_root) -> Result<SharedDb>` and call it from both.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs` — public `leader_shutdown_flag()`, `heartbeat_lease()`, `step_down()` lack doc comments (all public items must be documented).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs` — public `elect_as_follower_only()`, `nonce()`, `heartbeat()` lack doc comments.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` — `\"workspace mutex poisoned\"` literal repeated 4+ times; hoist to a `const`. Getter `get_tool_registry()` should drop the `get_` prefix per Rust API convention → `tool_registry()`.
+
+### Nits (engine fleet — non-blocking)
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs` — test magic numbers 4242 (foreign PID), 7777 (foreign nonce) should be named constants (`SIMULATED_FOREIGN_PID`, `SIMULATED_FOREIGN_NONCE`).
+
